revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Itahato-jinja]][[ja:伊多波刀神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊多波刀神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Itahato Shrine | native_name = 伊多波刀神社 | image = Itahato3.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Itahato Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.276252|136.955157}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Itahato Shrine'''|伊多波刀神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|ja|尾張国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 79 ** P155: {{ill|Q11579597|ltq=Q11579597|qid=Q11579597|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579597)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039524|ltq=Q135039524|qid=Q135039524|1=Takamuno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Kasugai, Aichi|lt=Kasugai|ar|كاسوغاي (آيتشي)|arz|كاسوجاى|azb|کاسوقای، آیچی|be|Касугаі|bg|Касугаи|ceb|Kasugai-shi|ce|Касугаи|ckb|کاسوگای، ئایچی|cs|Kasugai (Aiči)|de|Kasugai|en|Kasugai, Aichi|eo|Kasugai|es|Kasugai|eu|Kasugai (Aichi)|fa|کاسوگای|fi|Kasugai|fr|Kasugai|ga|Kasugai|gl|Kasugai|hy|Կասուգաի|id|Kasugai, Aichi|it|Kasugai|ja|春日井市|javoyage|春日井市|kk|Касугаи|ko|가스가이시|lt|Kasugajis|mdf|Касугаи|mg|Kasugai|mn|Касүгаи (Айчи)|mzn|کاسوگای، آیچی|nl|Kasugai|no|Kasugai|pl|Kasugai|pnb|کسوگا‌‍‌ئ|pt|Kasugai|ro|Kasugai|ru|Касугаи|sh|Kasugai, Aichi|simple|Kasugai, Aichi|sr|Касугај|sv|Kasugai|tg|Касугаи|th|คาซูงาอิ|tl|Kasugai, Aichi|tr|Kasugai|tt|Касугаи|uk|Касуґай (Айті)|ur|کاسوگائی، ایچی|vi|Kasugai, Aichi|war|Kasugai, Aichi|zh_classical|春日井市|zh_min_nan|Kasugai-chhī|zh_yue|春日井|zh|春日井市|qid=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.276252|136.955157}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Itahato3.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181419 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q3125455|ltq=Q3125455|qid=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いたはとじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 253694974 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7180005008056 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 01225394 == Commons category (P373) == * Itahato-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県春日井市上田楽町3454', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Itahato-jinja}} [[JA:伊多波刀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379270}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Itahato Shrine |画像 = [[File:Main hall of Itahato-jinja - 3.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|qid=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}} Kamidairaku-chō 3454 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto, Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 42 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|Q3125455|qid=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} |別名 = Hachimangū
Dairaku no Ōhachimangū |例祭 = 2nd Monday of October |神事 = {{ill|lt=Yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} (2nd Monday of October) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Itahato
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Big Stone lantern in Itahato-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Lantern at Itahato Shrine]] [[File:Sakura along approach to Itahato-jinja - 2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Approach]] [[File:Horse statue in Itahato-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{ill|lt=Sacred horse|Q11591047|qid=Q11591047|1=Shinme}} statue]] '''Itahato Shrine''' (いたはとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kamidairaku-chō|Q79377333|qid=Q79377333|1=Kamitaraga-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|qid=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Itahato Shrine” of Kasube District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. === Enshrined deities === The principal deities are {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyūga myth)|Q3082751|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Three Deities|1=UNKNOWN}}.” Also merged are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izunome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and Hondawake-no-Mikoto (same as Hondawake-no-Mikoto). === History === Shrine tradition says it was founded in year 42 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, but exact founding year is unknown. Until the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called '''Hachimangū'''. The oldest {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} from {{ill|lt=1523|Q6302|qid=Q6302|1=1523}} ({{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|qid=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} 3) reads “Hachimangū main shrine.” In the early-Edo '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}},' it appears as “Dairaku Village ... Hachiman ... held by village negi Sukedayū.” In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}, it was the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Dairaku Village in {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|qid=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, and revered as the general {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of 17 villages of Ajioka-shō. Traditions include donation of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shuinchi|1=UNKNOWN}} and three sets of {{ill|lt=armor|Q3671179|qid=Q3671179|1=Japanese armour}} by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} for military fortune; from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} onward it prospered as a {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} shrine attracting {{ill|lt=samurai houses|Q2927809|qid=Q2927809|1=Buke}}, and with donations by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} had 5 chō 8 tan of shrine land in the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, but this was all confiscated by {{ill|lt=land survey|Q1092846|qid=Q1092846|1=kenchi}}. Until early modern times there was {{ill|lt=Jōnen-ji (Kasugai)|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Jōnen-ji|1=Jōnen-ji}} (now defunct) as its {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. In mid-Edo, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified it with the Kasube District “Itahato Shrine” in the 'Engishiki Jinmyōchō,' and this became accepted theory. In the '{{ill|lt=Owari no Kuni Naijinmyōchō|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}},' it appears as “Junior Third Rank Itabato Tenjin.” The basis for Amano’s identification is unclear, but he considered a folk custom of drawing {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=pigeons|1=UNKNOWN}} on {{ill|lt=boards|Q114207382|qid=Q114207382|1=board}} and offering them to the deity as the origin of the name{{Sfn|津田正生|1978|p=84}}. {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} and others questioned this{{Sfn|津田正生|1978|p=84}}, and it has been pointed out that it may derive from association with the pigeon as {{ill|lt=divine messenger|Q10444973|qid=Q10444973|1=Shinshi}} of Hachiman'Shikinaisha Survey Report.'. Takeshi Shiga proposed a theory that it is a transformation of “Itahata,” meaning water-springing fields{{Sfn|加藤宏|1988|p=224}}. According to 'Aichi-ken Gun Chōson Aza-mei Chō' (1882), Dairaku Village had a {{ill|lt=koaza|Q5327509|qid=Q5327509|1=koaza}} called “Hatobe.” In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}} (Meiji 3) it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and just before abolition of the modern shrine rank system, on Jan. 10, {{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}} (Shōwa 21), it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Rites === The {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} was originally on old-calendar Aug. 15, later changed to Oct. 5, and now held on the second Monday of October. Until early modern times, {{ill|lt=yabusame|Q1886157|qid=Q1886157|1=Yabusame}} was dedicated at the annual festival; it is depicted in '{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|qid=Q11465290|1=Owari meisho zue}}' and said to have been the foremost ceremony in {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|qid=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}Kasugai City Hall, “[http://www.city.kasugai.lg.jp/22498/kanko/464/itadojinja.html Itahato Shrine]”.. Performers wore armor at Jōnen-ji and, after riding three {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=horses|1=UNKNOWN}} to worship, dedicated yabusame'Higashi Kasugai Gun-shi,' Higashi Kasugai District Office, Taishō 12.. After Meiji it declined and ceased; now only an armored warrior procession, horse-tower by men in unlucky ages, and warrior target-shooting by men in pre-unlucky year remain, but this rite was designated a city {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} on Apr. 14, {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} as “Yabusame Dedicated to Itahato Shrine”{{Cite web|ja|date=2011-04-01 |url=http://www.city.kasugai.lg.jp/bunka/bunkazai/shinai/shishitei.html |title=List of city-designated cultural properties |publisher=Kasugai City |accessdate=2013-05-27}}. On that day, local children also gather carrying {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|qid=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} and {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|qid=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}. === Precinct shrines === In the precincts are 14 shrines: {{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine in Kasugai|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsushima Shrine}}, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine, Toyouke Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Akiba Shrine, Hakusan Shrine, Atsuta Shrine, Atago Shrine, Tado Shrine, Sannō Shrine, Ōhirume Shrine, Sugawara Shrine, Goryō Shrine, Asama Shrine, and Takebe Shrine. Of these, Amaterasu Ōkami Shrine and Toyouke Shrine at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|qid=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance were formerly outside-precinct shrines. === Precincts === The cherry trees along the approach are a noted cherry-viewing spot in the cityAichi Prefecture Parks and Greenery Division, “[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/koen/keikanshigen/kasugai/021.html Cherry trees of Itahato Shrine, Kasugai City]”.. On the right side facing the main sanctuary is a lantern donated by Sasada Denzāemon and others{{Cite web |title=Tomichū Akitomo, “Following Sasada Denzāemon of Dairaku no Suya,” 'Kasugai Kyōdo-shi' No.7, Kasugai Local History Society |url=https://www.md.ccnw.ne.jp/hondacb/SUYA.htm |website= |access-date=2025-05-18}}. Sasada Denzāemon became adopted into the Sasada family in Dairaku village (his mother’s birthplace) and brewed vinegar. Later he moved to Fukuro-machi, Nagoya, and opened Suya. Further, the 3rd Sasada Denzāemon absorbed the Okada family (main Suya line) and took the name Suya Kanzaburō. In Meiji, the trademark “Marukan” was approved and registered as supplier to the Imperial Household Ministry. This became today’s Marukan Vinegar company. The main Sasada family line still remains in Kamidairaku-chō. === Cultural properties === * Dedicated yabusame - Kasugai City-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: from {{ill|lt=Kachigawa Station|Q5359249|qid=Q5359249|1=Kachigawa Station}} to {{ill|lt=Komaki Station|Q6428074|qid=Q6428074|1=Komaki Station}}, get off at “Machiya.” * Meitetsu Bus: from Kachigawa Station to {{ill|lt=Kasugai Municipal Hospital|Q11513541|qid=Q11513541|1=Kasugai Municipal Hospital}}, get off at “Machiya.” * Meitetsu Bus: from {{ill|lt=Kasugai Station (JR Tōkai)|Q5359327|qid=Q5359327|lt=Kasugai Station|1=Kasugai Station (JR Central)}} to Ōkusa, get off at “Machiya.” * Meitetsu Bus: from Kasugai Station to Komaki Station, get off at “Nishi-Machiya.” * {{ill|lt=Komaki Circulation Bus|Q11266831|qid=Q11266831|1=Komaki Jyunkai Bus}}: Tōkadai line from Komaki Station to Tōkadai Center-kami, get off at “Kamidairaku.” === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Hiroshi Itō, 'Historical Watching of Kasugai,' Hiroshi Itō, Jan. 1996, p.33 * 'Shikinaisha Survey Report' Vol.8 Tōkaidō 3, Kōgakkan University Press, 1989 * {{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue|Q11465290|qid=Q11465290|lt='Owari Meisho Zue' Vol.3 Itahato Shrine|1=Owari meisho zue}}, {{ill|lt=1844|Q7633|qid=Q7633|1=1844}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Takaki Village|Q11676377|qid=Q11676377|1=Takaki}} * {{ill|lt=Dairaku Fort|Q11577059|qid=Q11577059|1=Dairaku Fort}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kasugai Municipal Takaki Elementary School|Q97706299|qid=Q97706299|1=Kasugai Municipal Takaki Elementary School}} - southwest of shrine; has giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}. Opening line of school song mentions west of Itahato Shrine forest. * {{ill|lt=Chōfuku-ji (Kasugai)|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Chōfuku-ji|1=Chōfuku-ji}} * {{ill|lt=Shintoku-ji (Kasugai)|Q11502278|qid=Q11502278|lt=Shintoku-ji|1=Shintoku-ji Temple}} * {{ill|lt=Rinshō-in|Q11533081|qid=Q11533081|1=Rinshō-in Temple}} * {{ill|lt=Seto Shinkin Bank|Q11566535|qid=Q11566535|1=Seto Shinkin Bank}} Takaki Branch * {{ill|lt=Tōnō Shinkin Bank|Q11528017|qid=Q11528017|1=Tono Shinkin Bank}} Takaki Branch * {{ill|lt=Owari Wide-area Greenway|Q11465302|qid=Q11465302|1=Owari Pedestrian Parkway}} === External links === * [http://www.city.kasugai.lg.jp/22498/kanko/464/itadojinja.html Itahato Shrine] (Kasugai City Hall) * [http://www.pref.aichi.jp/koen/keikanshigen/kasugai/021.html Cherry trees of Itahato Shrine, Kasugai City] (Aichi Prefecture Parks and Greenery Division) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Itahato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Itahato Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:春日井市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kasugai, Aichi]]
